title: Iran War Live Updates: U.S. and Iran Exchange Fire Amid a Declared Truce
author: The New York Times
text: The U.S. military said it struck Iranian military targets after, it said, Iran fired on U.S. warships in the Strait of Hormuz. President Trump threatened more if Iran didn’t agree to a peace offer.

title: German Leaders Clash With Spy Chiefs Over Domestic Threat From Iran
author: Jim Tankersley, Philip Kaleta and Christopher F. Schuetze
text: Intelligence agents have privately warned of the potential of hybrid attacks from Iran-linked groups. But political leaders, including Chancellor Friedrich Merz, have publicly played down the risk.

title: Starmer Braces for Local Election Losses Amid New Era of UK Politics
author: Michael D. Shear and Stephen Castle
text: Polls predict historic losses for Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s Labour Party on Thursday as anti-immigrant Reform U.K. makes gains, and a new era of multiparty politics takes shape.

title: In Hungary, Voters Exposed the Limits of China’s Ties to Orban
author: Andrew Higgins and Lili Rutai
text: Beijing depended on Hungary’s outgoing leader, Viktor Orban, to gain a toehold in Europe. A giant battery factory proved a step too far.

title: Israel Says It Killed a Hezbollah Chief Near Beirut, Testing the Truce
author: Euan Ward
text: The strike was the first near the Lebanese capital since a cease-fire that has curbed fighting but not halted it. Washington is pushing for a lasting peace, hoping it will ease diplomacy with Iran.

title: Russia Ramps Up Threats Toward Ukraine Over Victory Day Parade
author: Cassandra Vinograd and Ivan Nechepurenko
text: Kremlin officials said a large strike on Kyiv would be “inevitable” if Ukraine disrupted the May 9 event, a prospect President Volodymyr Zelensky has appeared to allude to.

title: New NTSB Report Into Deadly China Eastern Crash Suggests Struggle in Cockpit
author: James Glanz
text: A report by the U.S. National Transportation Safety Board offers new details about the China Eastern Airlines crash in 2022, which killed all 132 people on board.

title: A Project in Europe Tested Whether Students Could Live Without Smartphones
author: Valeriya Safronova
text: For three weeks, thousands of children in Europe went without social media or their smartphones, or both. This is the diary of their digital detox.

title: China Hands Suspended Death Sentences to 2 Former Defense Ministers
author: Chris Buckley
text: Gen. Wei Fenghe and Gen. Li Shangfu are likely to spend the rest of their lives in prison after receiving suspended death sentences.

title: Man Arrested on Suspicion of Carrying Weapon Near Former Prince Andrew’s Home
author: Michael D. Shear
text: The police in Britain said that a suspect was in custody after reports he had behaved in an “intimidating manner.”

title: Trump Hosts Brazil’s President Lula at White House After Months of Ups and Downs
author: Adam B. Ellick
text: President Trump and President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, who have had a rocky relationship, met for talks on trade and other issues. But they skipped a planned joint appearance.

title: Australian Women and Children Linked to ISIS Fighters Return Home
author: Victoria Kim and Laura Chung
text: The police said two of them would face charges of crimes against humanity and the third would face a charge of belonging to a terrorist group.

title: Starmer Faces a Major Test in a Set of UK Elections: What to Know
author: Stephen Castle
text: Local elections in England, and parliamentary elections in Scotland and Wales, take place on Thursday. The governing Labour Party is expected to do very badly.

title: Here’s the latest.
author: Michael D. Shear
text: Here’s the latest.

title: China Sees a ‘Giant With a Limp’ as U.S. Drains Weapons on Iran War
author: David Pierson and Berry Wang
text: America’s ability to deter China in a war over Taiwan is weakened, Chinese analysts say, giving Beijing leverage in an upcoming summit with President Trump.

title: Many Russians in No Mood for Celebration on Kremlin’s Biggest Day of the Year
author: Valerie Hopkins and Nanna Heitmann
text: As the country heads into Victory Day, the economy is stalling, internet restrictions are growing and the Kremlin is fearing Ukrainian attacks.

title: U.S. to Review Mexican Consulates After Right-Wing Claims Against Them
author: Jack Nicas
text: The State Department said it would review all Mexican consulates in the United States, following claims in conservative media that the consulates interfere in politics.

title: Trump Says Cease-Fire Intact After Trade of Attacks in Strait of Hormuz
author: Erica L. Green
text: The president dismissed Iran’s firing on U.S. vessels in the strait as a mere “trifle.”

title: U.S. and Zambia Feud: Trump Health Aid Deal Stalls Over Critical Minerals
author: John Eligon and Rabecca Lungu
text: The tussle put a spotlight on the administration’s attempt to replace the United States Agency for International Development with a new, “America First” alternative.

title: U.S. and Iran Trade Strikes as Tensions in Strait of Hormuz Rise
author: Helene Cooper
text: The escalating attacks raise the threat that the cease-fire could break down.

title: Hantavirus Response Shows How Trump Cuts Have Compromised U.S. Preparedness
author: Apoorva Mandavilli
text: The Trump administration has slashed funding for infectious disease research and has far fewer employees, including disease detectives, to respond to outbreaks.

title: Employees With Medical Conditions Challenge C.D.C. In-Office Requirement
author: Apoorva Mandavilli
text: The health agency has been particularly strict in abolishing at-home work, overriding accommodations that were granted years before the pandemic.

title: Health Officials Race to Track Hantavirus Cruise Ship Outbreak but Predict ‘Limited’ Spread
author: Claire Moses, Aimee Ortiz and Rylee Kirk
text: A Dutch flight attendant and French national were among the latest to be tested in connection with a deadly outbreak on a cruise ship. But an analysis in South Africa showed no mutations in viral samples.

title: Her Self-Experiment with Drug Detox Almost Broke Her
author: Matt Richtel and Meridith Kohut
text: Against expert advice, people are using new and unpredictable synthetic drugs to experiment on themselves in hopes of becoming free of addiction.

title: American Factories Lag in Adopting A.I. This Drugmaker Is an Exception.
author: Farah Stockman
text: A Bristol Myers Squibb plant that makes cancer drugs was the only manufacturer in the U.S. recognized for innovation by the World Economic Forum this year.

title: F.D.A. Blocked Publication of Research Finding Covid and Shingles Vaccines Were Safe
author: Christina Jewett
text: The agency’s scientists and data contractors reviewed millions of patient records for studies that were pulled back before release.

title: A Medical Examiner Chases Down an Elusive Killer
author: Jan Hoffman and Brett Carlsen
text: Unusual opioids are infiltrating street drugs. Knoxville’s top forensic doctor is on the front lines, pressing to quickly identify the most lethal.

title: Kennedy Starts a Push to Help Americans Quit Antidepressants
author: Ellen Barry
text: The health secretary has long complained that Americans overuse psychiatric medications. New policies he is introducing aim to change that.

title: Supreme Court Temporarily Restores Access to Abortion Pill by Mail
author: Ann E. Marimow and Pam Belluck
text: A lower-court ruling had reinstated a Food and Drug Administration requirement that patients visit a health care provider in person to obtain mifepristone.

title: Hantavirus Outbreaks Are Rare, but They Aren’t Going Away and There’s No Cure
author: Adeel Hassan
text: Since the family of rodent-borne infections were identified in the 1950s, they have turned up all over the world.

title: What Is Hantavirus, Which Is Linked to the Deaths of 3 People Aboard a Cruise Ship?
author: Livia Albeck-Ripka, Hank Sanders and Michael Levenson
text: Three people aboard a cruise ship sailing the Atlantic Ocean have died from what health officials suspect were hantavirus infections.

title: A Long, Strange Trip: How the G.O.P. Came to Embrace Psychedelic Drugs
author: Andrew Jacobs
text: For decades, conservatives were adamantly opposed to the use of drugs like psilocybin and LSD. Now, the Trump administration has made a sharp pivot.

title: Supreme Court Asked to Restore Access to Abortion Pill by Mail
author: Ann E. Marimow and Pam Belluck
text: A federal appeals court temporarily halted a Food and Drug Administration regulation that has greatly expanded access to the abortion pill mifepristone.

title: What to Know About Orphines, a New Class of Deadly Opioids
author: Jan Hoffman
text: The drugs are 10 times more dangerous than fentanyl. They are showing up in street drugs in the South and the Midwest, and will most likely spread to other regions.

title: 3 Dead in Hantavirus Outbreak Aboard Cruise Ship, W.H.O. Says
author: Alexandra E. Petri and Livia Albeck-Ripka
text: One case of hantavirus infection has been confirmed in a laboratory, the organization said on Sunday. There are five additional suspected cases.

title: 3 Medical Routines That Older People May Not Need
author: Paula Span
text: Some screenings and treatments no longer make sense for patients as they age. Researchers have just added a few more to the list.

title: N.I.H. Reinstates Employee Put on Leave After Criticizing Trump Research Cuts
author: Sheryl Gay Stolberg
text: Jenna Norton had filed a whistle-blower complaint claiming that the agency leadership had retaliated against her.

title: F.D.A. Grants Early Access to Promising Drug for Pancreatic Cancer
author: Rebecca Robbins and Gina Kolata
text: Patients with one of the deadliest cancers have been pleading for an unapproved treatment that may prolong their lives.

title: Obamacare Enrollment Drops Sharply as Costs Rise
author: Reed Abelson and Margot Sanger-Katz
text: Americans can’t afford the higher health insurance premiums that resulted from Republicans’ refusal to extend federal tax credits.

title: Trump Picks Nicole Saphier for Surgeon General After Withdrawing Casey Means Nomination
author: Sheryl Gay Stolberg and Dani Blum
text: Dr. Means’s nomination had stalled in part over her views on vaccines. The president said he was instead nominating Dr. Nicole B. Saphier, a radiologist at Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center.
